title: Senator Shannon Grove ( R-Bakersfield ) is proud to announce Senate Bill 1203 and Senate Constitutional Amendment 9 , companion measures that will establish the Education Flex Acco

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Senate Bill 1203 and Senate Constitutional Amendment 9 aim to improve K-12 education in California by providing parents and students with greater choice in selecting schools.",
    "Under the current education system in California, students are assigned to schools based on geographic location, limiting options for families seeking alternative educational opportunities.",
    "Despite increased spending per student, California continues to rank behind the national average in academic achievement, with disparities in performance among students of color.",
    "Senate Bill 1203 and SCA 9 will create the Education Flex Account, empowering parents to choose accredited schools that best fit their child's needs.",
    "Students will have access to $8,000 through the Education Flex Account and $16,000 through the Special Education Flex Account for tuition and other education expenses.",
    "Any unused funds from the Education Flex Account will be returned to the General Fund to support K-12 education the following year.",
    "Public education in the United States is failing children and not preparing them to be civically minded adults in the 21st-century economy.",
    "The National Assessment of Educational Progress consistently ranks California’s students in the bottom tiers of academic achievement and lower than the national average for most grade levels.",
    "Giving parents more autonomy and choice in their children's education is crucially important.",
    "The Education Flex Account Act of 2024 represents a significant stride towards a more child-centered and effective approach to K-12 education."
  ]
}
```
